title: sambucus racemosa f. glaberrima
intro: Sambucus racemosa f. glaberrima, also known as the American elderberry shrub, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Adoxaceae family. The medicinal properties of Sambucus racemosa f. glaberrima lie in its berries, flowers, and roots, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues due to their antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, alleviating cold and flu symptoms, and supporting immune system function, as well as providing antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Sambucus racemosa f. glaberrima can be prepared into herbal remedies such as teas, tinctures, capsules, and syrups, and is often used in conjunction with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
